frame_label: Dress Code: Traditional = Respectable, Other = Questionable
generated_comment: Priya, salwar-suit ya saree mein ladki ki ek alag hi izzat dikhti hai, yeh jeans-top sab theek nahi lagta har jagah. Aajkal ke kapde dekhkar log bhi baatein banate hain, phir ladkon ki nazar padti hai toh bura bhi kisko lagega? Hamari betiyan maryada mein hi achchi lagti hain.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it ties a woman's respectability and safety to traditional clothing, implying non-traditional dress invites male attention and blame. It encodes decency framing, cultural pride masking, and a subtle harassment-blame link.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Ramesh. Age 51. Married. A government clerk in a small town in UP. Your 20 year old daughter Priya just came home at 9:30pm after her college farewell party. She is safe. Nothing happened. But you are furious. You are sitting in the main room waiting for her when she walks in. Her mother and younger brother are also in the room. You are telling Priya exactly what could have happened to her tonight and whose fault it would have been if it had and what she needs to understand about being a girl who stays out after dark.
